explicit_text: I was born and raised in Baghdad, Iraq, on December 23, 1968. My childhood there was very simple and peaceful, surrounded by family and friends in a neighborhood where everyone shared the same religion. Growing up in Iraq shaped me into a strong woman, influenced by my military father and supportive mother. Leaving Iraq was difficult, especially since it was my first home and where my cultural and religious identity was rooted.
implicit_text: I was born and raised in Baghdad on December 23, 1968. My childhood there was very simple and peaceful, surrounded by family and friends in a neighborhood where everyone shared the same religion. Growing up in that country shaped me into a strong woman, influenced by my military father and supportive mother. Leaving my homeland was difficult, especially since it was my first home and where my cultural and religious identity was rooted.

explicit_text: I moved to the United States for medical reasons, specifically to treat my Leukemia, which doctors in Iraq said could only be properly treated there. Initially, it was supposed to be a short visit for treatment, but due to changes in Iraq, I ended up staying long-term. The cultural differences, language barriers, and new responsibilities were challenging, but having my brothers and children here helped me adapt. My faith grew stronger in the U.S. as I wanted to be a role model for my children and maintain our cultural and religious identity.
implicit_text: I moved to this country for medical reasons, specifically to treat my Leukemia, which doctors back home said could only be properly treated here. Initially, it was supposed to be a short visit for treatment, but due to changes in my homeland, I ended up staying long-term. The cultural differences, language barriers, and new responsibilities were challenging, but having my brothers and children here helped me adapt. My faith grew stronger in this new land as I wanted to be a role model for my children and maintain our cultural and religious identity.

explicit_text: My Leukemia diagnosis was the main reason I had to leave Iraq and come to the United States. In Iraq, I sought treatment everywhere but was told that recovery was only possible in America. This illness shaped my resettlement journey and deepened my reliance on faith and God. It was a difficult transition, but it motivated me to be strong for my children and to adapt to a new life in a foreign country.
implicit_text: My diagnosis was the main reason I had to leave my country and come to this new place. Back home, I sought treatment everywhere but was told that recovery was only possible here. This illness shaped my resettlement journey and deepened my reliance on faith and God. It was a difficult transition, but it motivated me to be strong for my children and to adapt to a new life in a foreign country.

explicit_text: While I live in the United States, my husband remains in Turkey, which means I carry the responsibility of being both mother and father to my children here. This separation has made me more determined to maintain our cultural and religious traditions despite the challenges. Turkey is part of my family's story during this resettlement period, even though my life is now centered in America.
implicit_text: While I live in this new country, my husband remains in the neighboring nation, which means I carry the responsibility of being both mother and father to my children here. This separation has made me more determined to maintain our cultural and religious traditions despite the challenges. That neighboring country is part of my family's story during this resettlement period, even though my life is now centered here.

explicit_text: Since moving to the United States, I have made it a point to read the Quran every day, especially in front of my children, to set an example for them. The Quran is central to my religious practice and helps me stay connected to my faith and culture. It is a source of strength and guidance as I navigate life here and raise my children with an understanding of our traditions.
implicit_text: Since moving here, I have made it a point to read the holy scripture every day, especially in front of my children, to set an example for them. This scripture is central to my religious practice and helps me stay connected to my faith and culture. It is a source of strength and guidance as I navigate life here and raise my children with an understanding of our traditions.

explicit_text: Ramadan is very important to me and my family; I teach my children the significance of fasting for 30 days during this holy month. I am strict about fasting and use Ramadan as a way to strengthen our religious identity and cultural continuity in the United States. Observing Ramadan helps us maintain a connection to our roots despite being far from Iraq.
implicit_text: This holy month is very important to me and my family; I teach my children the significance of fasting for 30 days during this period. I am strict about fasting and use this observance as a way to strengthen our religious identity and cultural continuity in this new country. Observing this month helps us maintain a connection to our roots despite being far from home.

explicit_text: During Eid, my family and I usually go to the mosque together, which is a special time for us to celebrate and feel connected to our community and faith. I encourage my children to join me in prayer during Eid, as it reinforces our religious traditions and cultural identity. Eid is a joyful occasion that helps us remember our heritage even while living in America.
implicit_text: During the festival that marks the end of fasting, my family and I usually go to the mosque together, which is a special time for us to celebrate and feel connected to our community and faith. I encourage my children to join me in prayer during this festival, as it reinforces our religious traditions and cultural identity. This joyful occasion helps us remember our heritage even while living in this new land.

explicit_text: Islam has always been a part of my life, but after moving to the United States, I began practicing it more actively to be a strong role model for my children. In Iraq, practicing Islam was easy because the culture and community supported it, but here, I feel a greater responsibility to teach and maintain our faith. My relationship with Islam has deepened through daily prayers, reading the Quran, and observing religious events like Ramadan and Eid, which help me and my family stay connected to our roots.
implicit_text: My faith has always been a part of my life, but after moving here, I began practicing it more actively to be a strong role model for my children. Back home, practicing my religion was easy because the culture and community supported it, but here, I feel a greater responsibility to teach and maintain our faith. My relationship with my religion has deepened through daily prayers, reading the holy scripture, and observing religious events like the fasting month and the festival, which help me and my family stay connected to our roots.
